agent_output:
# Domain Analysis — E-Commerce Order & Inventory Rules

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context

This encodes the core purchasing lifecycle of an e-commerce platform: from cart management through checkout, payment, and post-sale refund. The rules govern **inventory reservation**, **promotional pricing**, **order immutability**, and **refund eligibility** — all critical to revenue integrity and customer trust.

### Ubiquitous Language

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Cart** | A transient, uncommitted collection of items a customer intends to purchase. Holds no inventory reservation. |
| **Checkout** | The process initiated when a customer commits to purchasing cart contents. Triggers inventory reservation. |
| **Inventory Reservation** | A time-bound hold on inventory units, created at checkout start, guaranteeing availability during payment. Expires after 15 minutes without payment. |
| **Reservation Window** | The 15-minute period from checkout initiation during which reserved inventory is held. |
| **Abandoned Checkout** | A checkout where no payment confirmation is received within the reservation window. |
| **Promotion** | A seller-defined pricing discount applied to a specific product (e.g., "20% off Widget X"). At most one may be active per product at any time. |
| **Discount Code** | A buyer-supplied code that applies a discount at the order level. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing. |
| **Active Promotion** | A promotion currently in effect for a product (not expired, not superseded). |
| **Order** | An immutable record of a completed purchase, created upon payment confirmation. |
| **Payment Confirmation** | The event marking successful payment processing. After this event, the order is frozen. |
| **Delivery Date** | The date the order is confirmed as delivered to the customer. Starts the refund eligibility clock. |
| **Refund Window** | The 30-calendar-day period after delivery during which a refund may be requested. |
| **Listed Product** | A product visible in the catalog. Visibility does NOT imply purchasability. |
| **Available Inventory** | Total stock minus all active reservations. Only products with available inventory > 0 can enter checkout. |

### Bounded Context

**Order & Fulfillment Context** — spanning Cart, Checkout, Payment, Inventory Reservation, Promotions/Discounts, and Refund Eligibility. Touches the **Catalog Context** (product listing, inventory counts) and **Pricing Context** (promotions, discount codes) at their boundaries.

---

## Business Rules

| # | Rule | Invariant | Testable Assertion |
|---|---|---|---|
| **BR-1** | Inventory is NOT reserved when items are added to cart — only when checkout begins. | ✅ true | Adding an item to a cart must not decrement available inventory or create a reservation record. |
| **BR-2** | A checkout that receives no payment within 15 minutes is abandoned, and all its inventory reservations are released. | ✅ true | After 15 minutes with no payment event, reserved units must be returned to available inventory and the checkout marked abandoned. |
| **BR-3** | A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time. | ✅ true | At no point in time may two promotion records with overlapping active date ranges exist for the same product. |
| **BR-4** | Discount codes and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive per order — only one discount mechanism applies. | ✅ true | An order line benefiting from a product promotion must reject application of a discount code, and vice versa. The system must enforce exactly zero or one discount mechanism per order. |
| **BR-5** | Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed. | ✅ true | Any attempt to alter line items, quantities, shipping address, or pricing on an order with status "paid" or beyond must be rejected. |
| **BR-6** | A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog. | ✅ true | Checkout initiation must fail for any line item whose product has available inventory ≤ 0 at reservation time. |
| **BR-7** | Refund requests must be submitted within 30 calendar days of the delivery date. | ❌ false (policy constraint, not a system invariant — may be overridden by customer service) | A refund request submitted on day 31+ post-delivery must be rejected by the standard flow. |

> **Why BR-7 is not a hard invariant:** Refund windows are typically business policy enforced at the application layer, with deliberate override paths for customer service escalations, regulatory requirements, or goodwill exceptions. The other rules (BR-1 through BR-6) are structural invariants whose violation would cause data corruption, overselling, or financial inconsistency.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### AC-1: Cart does not reserve inventory

| | |
|---|---|
| **Given** | A product with 5 units of available inventory |
| **When** | A customer adds 3 units to their cart |
| **Then** | Available inventory remains 5; no reservation record exists for this cart |

### AC-2: Checkout reserves inventory

| | |
|---|---|
| **Given** | A cart with 3 units of Product A (available inventory = 5) |
| **When** | The customer initiates checkout |
| **Then** | A reservation for 3 units is created; available inventory for Product A becomes 2; the reservation has a 15-minute expiry |

### AC-3: Abandoned checkout releases reservation

| | |
|---|---|
| **Given** | A checkout with a reservation for 3 units of Product A, initiated 15 minutes ago |
| **When** | No payment confirmation has been received |
| **Then** | The reservation is released; available inventory for Product A increases by 3; checkout status becomes "abandoned" |

### AC-4: Single active promotion per product

| | |
|---|---|
| **Given** | Product A already has an active promotion ("Summer Sale — 20% off") |
| **When** | An admin attempts to create a second promotion for Product A with overlapping dates |
| **Then** | The system rejects the second promotion with a clear error; the original promotion remains unchanged |

### AC-5: Discount code and promotion are mutually exclusive

| | |
|---|---|
| **Given** | A cart containing Product A which has an active 20% promotion |
| **When** | The customer applies discount code "SAVE10" at checkout |
| **Then** | The system rejects the discount code (or warns the customer that only one discount mechanism applies) and does not stack both discounts |

### AC-6: Order immutability after payment

| | |
|---|---|
| **Given** | An order with status "paid" |
| **When** | Any actor attempts to modify line items, quantities, or pricing |
| **Then** | The modification is rejected; the order state remains unchanged |

### AC-7: Zero-inventory product cannot be purchased

| | |
|---|---|
| **Given** | Product B is listed in the catalog with available inventory = 0 |
| **When** | A customer attempts to initiate checkout with Product B in their cart |
| **Then** | Checkout fails for Product B with a clear "out of stock" error; no reservation is created |

### AC-8: Refund within 30-day window

| | |
|---|---|
| **Given** | An order delivered on March 1st |
| **When** | The customer requests a refund on March 30th (day 29) |
| **Then** | The refund request is accepted and processed |

### AC-9: Refund outside 30-day window

| | |
|---|---|
| **Given** | An order delivered on March 1st |
| **When** | The customer requests a refund on April 1st (day 31) |
| **Then** | The refund request is rejected with a clear "refund window expired" message |

---

### Edge Cases

| Scenario | Expected Behavior |
|---|---|
| **Two customers checkout the last unit simultaneously** | Only one reservation succeeds; the second receives an "out of stock" error. Reservation must be atomic (optimistic lock or compare-and-swap on inventory count). |
| **Payment arrives at exactly 15:00.000** | Define boundary as exclusive — payment at exactly 15 minutes is treated as expired. The system should use "less than," not "less than or equal." Document this and be consistent. |
| **Customer adds item to cart, then inventory drops to zero before checkout** | Checkout initiation must re-validate inventory. Cart contents are aspirational, not guaranteed. |
| **Promotion ends mid-checkout** | The price locked at checkout initiation should be honored for the duration of the reservation window. Price changes should not retroactively affect in-flight checkouts. |
| **Discount code applied, then a promotion is activated on the product before payment** | The discount mechanism chosen at checkout start is the one that applies. Do not switch mid-checkout. |
| **Customer requests refund on exactly day 30** | Day 30 is within the window. Boundary: refund eligible if `delivery_date + 30 days >= request_date`. |
| **Product inventory restored after abandoned checkout — race with new checkout** | Released inventory must be immediately available. No stale reads — released reservations should be fully committed before inventory becomes bookable. |
| **Order paid, then payment reversed by processor (chargeback)** | Order immutability (BR-5) applies to customer/merchant modifications. Chargebacks are an external financial event handled in a separate Payments bounded context; they do not "modify" the order but create a compensating event. |
| **Which discount wins when both are present?** | The rules say they are mutually exclusive, but do not specify priority. **Recommendation:** Apply the mechanism that gives the customer the better price, OR require the customer to choose. This is an open business decision that needs product team clarification. |

---

## Business Impact

| Dimension | Assessment |
|---|---|
| **Financial** | Correct inventory reservation prevents overselling (direct revenue loss from cancellations and compensation). Mutual exclusivity of discounts prevents unintended margin erosion from stacked discounts. The 15-minute reservation window balances conversion (giving customers time) against inventory lock-up cost. |
| **Operational** | Reservation expiry automation reduces manual intervention. Order immutability simplifies fulfillment pipeline — no mid-flight mutations to reconcile. Single-promotion constraint simplifies pricing engine and prevents conflicting marketing campaigns. |
| **User** | Cart-without-reservation is customer-friendly (no "held in someone else's cart" frustration), but risks disappointment at checkout if inventory drops. Clear error messaging at checkout boundary is critical for trust. |
| **Risk** | Race conditions on inventory reservation are the highest technical risk — can cause overselling. Clock skew on reservation expiry could release inventory prematurely or hold it too long. The 30-day refund policy without a hard invariant allows customer service flexibility but creates abuse surface. |

### Stakeholders Affected

| Group | Impact |
|---|---|
| **Customers** | Experience depends on clear feedback at checkout when inventory is gone; frustration if discount stacking expectation is violated without explanation |
| **Merchandising / Marketing** | Must coordinate promotions — one-at-a-time constraint requires planning; need tooling to see active promotions before creating new ones |
| **Finance / Accounting** | Order immutability simplifies revenue recognition; refund window bounds liability exposure |
| **Customer Service** | Need an override path for BR-7 (refund window); will handle edge-case escalations around discount mutual exclusivity |
| **Warehouse / Fulfillment** | Depend on reservation accuracy — overselling creates costly expedited shipping and cancellation workflows |

---

## Confidence Assessment

| | |
|---|---|
| **Confidence Level** | **82%** |

### High Confidence Areas
- Inventory reservation lifecycle (BR-1, BR-2, BR-6) — unambiguous rules, clear state transitions
- Order immutability (BR-5) — straightforward invariant
- Single promotion constraint (BR-3) — clear cardinality rule
- Refund window (BR-7) — clear policy

### Low Confidence Areas
- **Discount conflict resolution priority** — rules state mutual exclusivity but do not specify which mechanism wins or who chooses. Needs product team clarification.
- **Reservation boundary precision** — "15 minutes" needs explicit inclusive/exclusive boundary definition.
- **Refund window override path** — business says 30 days, but real-world operations almost certainly need exceptions. Scope of override authority is undefined.
- **Promotion timing during in-flight checkout** — what happens when a promotion starts or ends while a customer is in the reservation window? Price-lock semantics are not specified.
- **Partial checkout failure** — if a cart has 3 products and only 1 is out of stock, does the entire checkout fail or can the customer proceed with the available items?

### Assumptions Made
1. "Available inventory" means total stock minus active reservations (not total stock minus sold units, which would ignore reservations).
2. The 30-day refund window is calendar days, not business days.
3. "Cannot be modified" means the entire order record, not just line items — includes shipping address, pricing, and metadata.
4. Discount mutual exclusivity applies at the **order** level, not per line item. (If per-line-item, some items could use promo and others could use a code — this would be a different rule.)
5. "Checkout begins" is a discrete event, not a gradual process — there is a single moment when reservation is created.

---

## Rejection Reasons

None at this stage — these are requirements, not an implementation to evaluate. However, **any implementation that allows the following would be rejected:**

| Violation | Business Rule Broken |
|---|---|
| Cart add decrements inventory | BR-1 — inventory reserved too early, punishes browsing customers |
| Stacked discounts (promotion + code) applied to same order | BR-4 — margin erosion, pricing integrity destroyed |
| Order line items editable after payment | BR-5 — fulfillment pipeline integrity, revenue recognition |
| Purchase succeeds with zero inventory | BR-6 — overselling, customer trust, operational cost |
| Reservation held indefinitely without payment | BR-2 — inventory hostage, denial-of-service on stock |
